senses.definition: Small computer programs that run inside a larger program and perform a single simple task.
examples: The website uses Java applets to display interactive charts.
You can add applets to your desktop to show the weather or the time.
Older browsers sometimes have trouble loading certain applets.

senses.definition: An old word for a woman who sells apples in the street or in a market.
examples: The applewife called out to people passing by her stall.
In the story, a poor applewife helps the lost child find his way home.

senses.definition: An old word for a woman who sells apples, especially in the street.
examples: The applewoman sat on the corner from morning till night.
An old applewoman watched the children play in the square.

senses.definition: Wood from an apple tree, often used for smoking food or for furniture.
examples: They used applewood to smoke the bacon.
The table was made from solid applewood.
Applewood gives the cheese a sweet, smoky flavor.

senses.definition: Having the taste, smell, or quality of apples.
examples: The cider had a fresh, appley flavor.
This perfume is light and appley.
She baked a cake with a sweet, appley smell.

senses.definition: Able to be used in a particular situation; suitable for practical use.
examples: These rules are not appliable to every case.
The theory is hardly appliable in real life.
We must check if the method is appliable here.

senses.definition: The quality of being able to be used in a particular situation.
examples: We discussed the appliableness of these findings to other groups.
The appliableness of the policy remains uncertain.

senses.definition: In a way that can be used or is suitable in a particular situation.
examples: The model can be appliably extended to other contexts.
These guidelines may not be appliably adapted everywhere.

senses.definition: A piece of electrical equipment used in the home, such as a fridge or a washing machine.
examples: The kitchen is full of modern appliances.
We bought a new appliance to dry our clothes.
Always unplug the appliance after use.

senses.definition: A piece of equipment fixed to the body to help a medical or dental problem.
examples: The dentist fitted her with a dental appliance.
He wears a special appliance to support his back.

senses.definition: Pieces of electrical equipment used in homes, especially in the kitchen.
examples: The store sells all kinds of home appliances.
Energy-efficient appliances can lower your bills.
They decided to replace their old appliances.

senses.definition: An old or rare word meaning a person who makes a request or appeal.
examples: The appliant waited humbly for the judge's decision.
Every appliant was treated with respect.

senses.definition: Different ways or situations in which something can be used or is relevant.
examples: We compared the applicabilities of two research methods.
The study explores the applicabilities of this model across cultures.

senses.definition: The degree to which something can be used in a particular situation or is related to it.
examples: We need to check the applicability of these results to real-life settings.
Questions were raised about the applicability of the new law.
The report discusses the applicability of the theory in practice.

senses.definition: That can be used in a particular situation or is related to it.
examples: These rules are applicable to all students.
The discount is only applicable on purchases over fifty dollars.
The method is not applicable in every case.

senses.definition: The quality of being suitable or relevant in a particular situation.
examples: We doubted the applicableness of the theory to human behavior.
The applicableness of such measures is still debated.

senses.definition: In a way that is suitable or relevant to a particular situation.
examples: The law should be applicably enforced across all regions.
These principles can be applicably used in many fields.

senses.definition: The action or state of applying for something, especially a job or position.
examples: Her applicancy for the role was supported by strong references.
We will review your applicancy and contact you soon.

senses.definition: A person who formally asks for something, especially a job, place at a school, or official document.
examples: Each applicant must send a cover letter and a CV.
There were more than two hundred applicants for the position.
Every applicant will receive a response by email.

senses.definition: People who formally ask for something, especially jobs, places at a school, or official documents.
examples: Over three hundred applicants applied for the scholarship.
All applicants will be informed of the result.
The company interviewed ten applicants.

senses.definition: To put or lay something on another thing, especially to attach or apply it closely.
examples: The craftsman applicated the gold leaf to the frame.
Tiny pieces of glass were applicated to the surface.

senses.definition: A formal, usually written, request for something such as a job, a place at a school, or permission.
examples: She filled in an online application for the job.
The deadline for applications is next Friday.
Your application for a visa has been approved.

senses.definition: The practical use of an idea, method, or piece of equipment.
examples: This research has important practical applications.
We discussed the application of the theory to real problems.
New applications of AI are appearing every year.

senses.definition: A computer program, especially one designed to do a particular job.
examples: You need to install this application on your phone.
The application crashed while I was saving the file.
This application helps you track your expenses.

senses.definition: The act of putting something such as paint, cream, or pressure onto a surface.
examples: Regular application of the cream will improve your skin.
The application of heat can speed up the process.
Careful application of paint is required.

senses.definition: Formal requests for something such as jobs, school places, or permission.
examples: We received over fifty applications for the role.
Late applications will not be accepted.
All applications must be completed in English.

senses.definition: Ways in which an idea, method, or technology can be used in practice.
examples: The technology has many potential applications.
We looked at different applications of the model.
These findings have practical applications in education.

senses.definition: Computer programs designed to perform particular tasks.
examples: You can download new applications from the store.
Many applications run in the background on your phone.
These applications are easy for beginners to use.

senses.definition: Relating to putting something into practical use.
examples: The course has a strongly applicative focus.
We need more applicative research in this area.

senses.definition: In a way that is related to practical use or application.
examples: The theory is applicatively demonstrated in this experiment.
These ideas can be applicatively extended to other fields.

senses.definition: A tool or device used to put a substance such as cream, makeup, or glue onto something.
examples: Use the sponge applicator to blend the foundation.
The cream comes with a small applicator.
A brush applicator makes the product easier to use.

senses.definition: In a way that concerns or relates to the act of applying something.
examples: The instructions are applicatorily detailed.
The tool is applicatorily designed for precision.

senses.definition: Tools or devices used to put a substance such as cream, makeup, or glue onto something.
examples: The kit includes several different applicators.
Disposable applicators are used for hygiene.
Makeup artists often carry many types of applicators.

senses.definition: Relating to the act of putting something into use or practice.
examples: The book provides applicatory examples of the theory.
We need applicatory skills, not only abstract knowledge.

senses.definition: Put something such as a cream, rule, or method onto a surface or into use.
examples: She applied the cream to her hands.
The teacher applied the new method in class.
We applied pressure to stop the bleeding.

senses.definition: Relating to the practical use of ideas and knowledge.
examples: He is studying applied mathematics.
Applied research often works with real-world problems.
The course has a strong focus on applied skills.

senses.definition: In a practical way or with a focus on real use.
examples: The topic was appliedly approached in the workshop.

senses.definition: A person or thing that applies something.
examples: The applier of the rules must be fair.
The cream comes with a built-in applier.

senses.definition: People or things that apply something.
examples: The appliers of the law must act responsibly.
Different appliers may interpret the guidelines differently.

senses.definition: Is true or relevant in a particular situation.
examples: This rule only applies to new students.
The discount applies if you spend more than fifty dollars.
What I said applies to everyone here.

senses.definition: Puts something such as a cream, paint, or rule into use.
examples: She applies the cream every night.
He applies pressure to the wound.
The company applies strict safety standards.

senses.definition: An old second person singular form of 'applies', used with 'thou' in older English.
examples: If thou appliest thy heart, thou shalt learn wisdom.
Thou appliest the law with mercy.

senses.definition: A small or young apple; an old or dialect word.
examples: He picked a tiny appling from the tree.
The basket was filled with green applings.

senses.definition: A decoration made by sewing a piece of cloth onto another piece of cloth.
examples: The dress had a floral applique on the front.
She learned how to make applique in her sewing class.
Applique can add interest to plain fabric.

senses.definition: To decorate something by sewing pieces of cloth onto it.
examples: The cushion was appliqued with bright shapes.
She appliqued flowers onto the quilt.

senses.definition: Decorated by sewing pieces of cloth onto a larger piece of cloth.
examples: She wore an appliqued skirt with colorful patterns.
The appliqued cushions brightened the room.

senses.definition: Sewing pieces of cloth onto another piece of cloth as a decoration.
examples: She spent the afternoon appliqueing shapes onto the blanket.
Appliqueing requires patience and attention to detail.

senses.definition: Decorations made by sewing pieces of cloth onto another piece of cloth.
examples: The dress was covered in lace appliques.
She cut out small appliques for the quilt.

senses.definition: The act of striking or pressing one thing against another; a rare term.
examples: The applosion of the surfaces created a tight seal.
A gentle applosion of the parts is enough.

senses.definition: Relating to or involving striking or pressing against something; rare.
examples: An applosive force holds the plates together.
The device uses applosive pressure to close.

senses.definition: To divide and assign land or property, especially for tax or rent; an old word.
examples: The estate was applotted among the tenants.
They applotted the land according to need.

senses.definition: The act of dividing and assigning land, property, or charges; an old term.
examples: The applotment of the tax burden was considered unfair.
They disputed the applotment of the land.

senses.definition: To make a formal request for something, such as a job or a place at a school.
examples: She wants to apply for a job abroad.
You must apply for a visa before you travel.
Many students apply to this university every year.

senses.definition: To put something such as a cream, paint, or rule onto a surface or into use.
examples: Apply the cream to clean, dry skin.
You should apply pressure to the wound.
The company applies strict safety rules.

senses.definition: To be true or relevant in a particular situation.
examples: This rule does not apply here.
The same idea applies to language learning.
The discount only applies to members.

senses.definition: To work hard and give serious attention to something.
examples: If you apply yourself, you will improve quickly.
She applied herself to her studies.
He never really applied himself at school.

senses.definition: Making a formal request for something, such as a job or a place at a school.
examples: She is applying for several jobs abroad.
They are applying to universities in Europe.

senses.definition: Putting something such as a cream, paint, or rule onto a surface or into use.
examples: She is applying the cream to her face.
We are applying new safety rules this year.

senses.definition: Giving serious attention or effort to something.
examples: He is really applying himself this term.
By applying yourself, you can pass the exam.

senses.definition: In a way that shows effort or practical use; a rare term.
examples: He worked applyingly on the project.

senses.definition: An old form meaning application or the act of applying something.
examples: Through careful applyment, he mastered the skill.
The applyment of these rules was strict.

senses.definition: A short written form of 'appointment'.
examples: I have a dentist appmt at 3 pm.
Please confirm your appmt by email.

senses.definition: In music, a note that does not belong to the main chord and is played quickly before a main note to make the melody more expressive.
examples: The singer added an appoggiatura before the final note.
Understanding how to play an appoggiatura is important in Baroque music.
The appoggiatura leans toward the main note and then resolves.

senses.definition: Notes in music that do not belong to the main chord and are played quickly before main notes to make the melody more expressive.
examples: The piece is full of appoggiaturas and other ornaments.
Students practiced playing appoggiaturas in different keys.
